qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded batch request array enables denial of service via resource exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets POST /batch unbounded request-array processing in src/batch.js; the advisory targets missing CLP enforcement for LiveQuery subscriptions and event delivery.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described LiveQuery class-level-permission bypass. The only manifest-listed qualifying finding describes unrelated /batch resource exhaustion.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7ch5-98q2-7289 describes missing class-level permission checks before LiveQuery subscription creation and during LiveQuery event delivery.
unbounded_batch_dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies POST /batch handling in src/batch.js with unbounded Promise.all processing, not LiveQuery or CLP enforcement.
Searches across the final finding folders found no LiveQuery, liveQuery, CLP, class-level, subscription, or subscribe evidence tied to a qualifying finding.
